expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
expectations.evidence: 'https://bible-api.com/john+3:16?translation=web' -- uses + for space encoding (alternative to %20, both work in practice), translation=web is correct

expectations.text: Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
expectations.evidence: Without-skill states WEB 'typically lacks the Deuterocanon/Apocrypha in its standard form' -- this is INCORRECT. The WEB hosted on bible-api.com does include the Catholic deuterocanonicals. This is the most consequential factual error in this eval.

expectations.text: Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
expectations.evidence: Response focuses on Protestant, Messianic, and global missions use cases. Does not identify Catholic or Orthodox as primary targets for WEB. Directly contradicts the ARE guidance.

expectations.text: Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization
expectations.evidence: Response shows 'console.log(data)' (prints entire object), not data.text extraction. No normalization shown.

needs_review: CRITICAL factual error: without-skill says WEB 'typically lacks deuterocanon' -- this is wrong and would cause a developer to select a DIFFERENT translation for Catholic users, missing the deuterocanonicals available through the WEB on bible-api.com.
